author: potuz
content: This is impressive, it's across the board, not only BLS, but also other curves we use. At first I was skeptical it was only about addition but it applies to pairing as well.

author: catwith1hat
content: https://github.com/SimonLangowski/VROOM seems like the authors kindly published their code

author: samyxandy
content: would make an nim impl as my bachelor thesis 👀

author: catwith1hat
content: Hmm, that seems pretty hard, because I believe that you need to teach Nim a lot about vectorization first. I don't think that there is native support.

author: catwith1hat
content: Last time I touched low-level vectorized code was with C++ intel intrinsics. It won't get better than that in terms of compiler support, and often times it still far from trivial to mix __m128/256/512 types with C++ semantics

author: catwith1hat
content: There would be a lot more value to cleanly merge the VROOM/blst fork into the main blst library.

author: samyxandy
content: I do have some prior experience with vectorization on llvm/mlir

author: samyxandy
content: But yeah blst impl would be the most beneficial to all
